Title: Cr3a, a candidate gene conferring fruit cracking resistance, was fine-mapped in an introgression line of Solanum lycopersicum L.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: SAMe; coniferyl alcohol; coniferin; L-phenylalanine; coumarin; 13-HPODE; 9-oxo-ODE
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Class: amino acid derivative; phenylpropanoid; fatty acid oxidation product
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Associated with fruit ripening, lignin/cell-wall related metabolism, and oxidative lipid metabolism linked to tomato fruit cracking resistance
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Protein kinase superfamily; Serine/Threonine protein kinase catalytic domain (STKc/S_TKc)
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Confers tomato fruit cracking resistance; overexpression of the resistant allele alleviates cracking, whereas knockout causes cracking susceptibility
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Higher at breaker and red ripe stages in resistant material
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: In resistant line 121B, expression is low at mature green and higher at breaker/red ripe; in susceptible line 776A, relatively high at mature green and lower later
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Fine mapping; qRT-PCR; transgenic overexpression; CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; transcriptome-metabolome integration
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fruit developmental stages: mature green, breaker, red ripe
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Cr3a was fine-mapped to a ~27.24-30 kb region on chromosome 3 and functionally identified as Sopen03g034650/Solyc03g115660.3 rather than the neighboring formin-like gene. 2. CRISPR knockout of Cr3a(650) in resistant tomato induced fruit cracking, while transgenic introduction into susceptible tomato alleviated cracking. 3. Multi-omics analysis linked Cr3a status to seven key metabolites and four pathways associated with crack resistance.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A validated gene for improving tomato fruit cracking resistance through marker-assisted breeding, introgression, transgenic deployment, or allele replacement/editing of elite fresh-market cultivars
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although multi-omics identified 13 downstream metabolism-related genes, these were part of the inferred regulatory network around Cr3a rather than the primary gene cloning target of the Results.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: SAMe; coniferyl alcohol; coniferin; L-phenylalanine; coumarin; 13-HPODE; 9-oxo-ODE
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Class: amino acid derivative; phenylpropanoid; fatty acid oxidation product
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Metabolites are associated with ethylene precursor metabolism, lignin/cell wall formation, and oxidized lipid metabolism contributing to fruit cracking resistance
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: CDK/CDKE-like protein kinase; Ser/Thr kinase catalytic domain (STKc/S_TKc)
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positive regulator of tomato fruit cracking resistance; resistant allele reduces cracking incidence
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Resistant allele is low at mature green and higher at breaker and red ripe stages
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: In 121B and OE line 776B, expression pattern resembles resistant type; knockout line 121A shows near loss of expression
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Fine mapping; gene overexpression in susceptible line; CRISPR/Cas9 knockout in resistant line; qRT-PCR; integrated transcriptome and metabolome profiling
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fruit developmental stage-dependent effects (mature green, breaker, red ripe)
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Among two positional candidates, only Cr3a(650) showed causal effects on fruit cracking resistance in gain- and loss-of-function tests. 2. Cr3a encodes a kinase and its allelic variation between S. pennellii and cultivated tomato correlates with resistance versus susceptibility. 3. Cr3a status is associated with coordinated shifts in phenylpropanoid, phenylalanine-related, cysteine/methionine, and linoleic acid metabolic networks.
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: MetK; ACS1_2_6; CAD; COMT; POD; UGT72E; pheA2; ADT; PAL; PTAL; beta-G; LOX2S; LOX1_5
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Coordinated regulation of fruit pericarp metabolic remodeling associated with phenylpropanoid biosynthesis, amino acid metabolism, ethylene precursor metabolism, and linoleic acid oxidation
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Associated with altered expression of 13 metabolism-related genes in the Cr3a knockout versus resistant background; direction varies by target and developmental stage
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Major cloned determinant of tomato fruit cracking resistance that reshapes key metabolite accumulation in fruit epidermis/pericarp
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Promoter contains cis-elements linked to MeJA, auxin, ABA, drought, low temperature, circadian regulation, MYBHv1 binding, and salicylic acid responsiveness
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Likely signaling-mediated regulation via protein phosphorylation affecting downstream metabolic gene expression
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value target for marker-assisted introgression and gene editing to improve tomato fruit cracking resistance while leveraging wild allele from S. pennellii
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Neighboring candidate Cr3a(660)/Sopen03g034660 was excluded by transgenic and knockout validation; mechanism remains inferred rather than direct kinase-substrate identification.
